title: Affirm Holdings Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Key Excerpts from Affirm Holdings Q4 2022 Earnings Call. Financial Performance: Affirm exceeded guidance across the board in Q4 fiscal 2022. The company achieved 77% GMV growth (89% excluding Peloton) and grew active consumers 96% year-over-year to 14 million. CEO Max Levchin stated the quarter capped off an exceptionally strong fiscal 2022 for Affirm. Credit Performance & Risk Management: Management acknowledged early signs of consumer stress in certain low-credit segments but maintained confidence in their ability to manage risk. Rather than dramatically slowing growth, Affirm made subtle adjustments through small optimizations while still achieving significant GMV growth. Michael Linford noted their allowance for credit losses declined for the third consecutive quarter to 6.2%. Growth Initiatives: Key growth drivers included: Inclusive credit targeting approximately 45 million Americans outside traditional credit infrastructure. Debit+ achieving 2.7 transactions per week with near-perfect early cohort retention. Rewards program launching in beta September 2022 to drive incremental transactions. International expansion with Affirm Canada posting 116% GMV growth and UK market investment planned. Fiscal 2023 Outlook: The company provided conservative guidance anticipating macroeconomic headwinds. GMV growth 32-42% to reach $20.5-$22 billion. Revenue $1.625-$1.725 billion (20-28% growth). Adjusted operating loss 6.5-4.5% of revenue, targeting sustained profitability by year-end.
